title: Star Wars (2015) 69
content:
{{Top|rwm|aor|title=''Star Wars'' (2015) 69}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars 69}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:StarWars2015-69.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'' 69 |writer=[[Greg Pak]] |penciller=[[Phil Noto]] |inker=Phil Noto |letterer=[[Clayton Cowles]] |colorist=Phil Noto |cover artist=Phil Noto |editor=[[Mark Paniccia]] |publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] |release date=[[July 24]], [[2019]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/71195/star_wars_2015_69|text=Star Wars (2015) #69}} |pages=24 |upc=759606081134 |timeline=c. [[2 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of ''Star Wars'' (2015) 69 to around [[2 ABY]]. |series=[[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'' (2015)]] |issue=69 }} '''''Star Wars'' 69''' is the sixty-ninth issue of the [[canon]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Greg Pak]], illustrated by [[Phil Noto]], and published on [[July 24]], [[2019]] by [[Marvel Comics]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''TRICKS AND TRAPS! The REBELS AND ROGUES epic continues as our heroes deploy their plans to trick their Imperial pursuers… and everything falls apart! CHEWIE and THREEPIO face moral quandaries — and mortal danger — when they learn the uninhabited planet they're supposed to destroy isn't quite so uninhabited. Will the FORCE be with LUKE in a bar fight? And who is DAR CHAMPION… and what does he have to do with Leia's past?'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=REBELS AND ROGUES
Part II |text=The evil Empire has dispatched countless probes to scour the cosmos in the hope of finding the Rebel Alliance. In response, the rebels have enacted a daring plan! While Luke Skywalker attempts to lead the Empire away from a rebel refueling station, Princess Leia Organa and smuggler Han Solo have been sent to the planet Lanz Carpo to destroy their enemies in the Carpo Crime Syndicate by convincing the Empire of collusion between the gang and the Rebellion. Meanwhile, Chewbacca and C-3PO were armed with explosives to set off a chain reaction that would destroy the uninhabited planet of K43—taking any nearby Star Destroyers with it. But K43 isn't as deserted as Chewie and C-3PO thought! And an old flame from Leia's past may complicate matters on Lanz Carpo....}} ==Plot summary== ===Political maneuvers and past tensions=== On the crime-ridden planet of [[Lanz Carpo]], District Advocate [[Dar Champion]] makes a public show of arresting three [[Red Carpo Guard|Red Carpo]] guards for extortion. A news reporter covers the event, and when asked if the charges will stick, Dar cryptically replies that he's just fighting to make the city safer. As the guards are booked, a droid arrives with a counterorder from a regional magistrate, and Dar explains to Leia that this is the routine on Lanz Carpo: he makes an arrest, and [[Crime lord|Boss]] [[Carpo]] bails them out. When Han asks what the point is, Dar responds that it is for the sake of "justice." Leia reveals to Han that Dar was her former boyfriend from her Regents' Training, a detail that Dar overhears. He brings up how Leia stood him up before a graduation dance, something he suspects was related to his father's scandal and the indictment of his family for embezzlement. Although his father was guilty, Dar says it took him years to clear his own name. Following the tense reunion, Han questions whether they can trust Dar, but Leia says they will complete their mission without his help. Dar did provide them with a pass to the [[Grand Regent]], which is full of Boss Carpo's thugs, so their infiltration mission to get to the comm center will be a challenge. ===Distractions and dangers=== Elsewhere, on [[Sergia]], Luke successfully cuts down an Imperial probe droid. He sends a message by hologram to a nearby rebel group, warning them that the planet is being searched for rebel bases and that they need to lay low while he handles the other Imperial pods. After hours of searching, he spots a nearby city and decides to head there. After entering an establishment, a fight breaks out, and Luke finds himself on the run with a woman who claims to know the Force. Meanwhile, on the volcanic planet [[Kakra|K43]], Chewbacca and C-3PO are confronted by the rock people who saved them. C-3PO's inability to communicate with them, combined with the imminent explosion of the detonators they planted, leads to a misunderstanding. The rock people become agitated and chase the pair back to the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]''. C-3PO expresses concern about destroying sentient life, but Chewbacca has already triggered the homing beacon, and a massive Star Destroyer appears in the atmosphere, deploying ships to the planet.
